channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Body found in same Austin lake where 6 other deceased individuals were found in 2024
item.description: A body was found in a Texas lake, the same site where other deceased individuals have been discovered in recent months, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top-ranking NYPD officer abruptly resigns amid sexual misconduct allegations
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The highest-ranking uniformed officer in the New York Police Department has resigned amid allegations he demanded sex from a subordinate in exchange for opportunities to earn extra pay.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Veteran who lost both legs shares his hopeful story for the holidays
item.description: One month later, on Thanksgiving Day, Gabe called Kayla with the devastating news that he had been injured by an improvised explosive device blast and med-evacuated out with one of his best friends. Both men lost their legs.
item.dc:creator: Kim Posey, NewsNation

item.title: Some sugary treats not as bad for you as others: Study
item.description: "The findings emphasize the adverse health effects of sweetened beverage consumption," researchers wrote.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: NJ rep. says it's alarming Feds can't bring down one drone
item.description: Chris Smith is disappointed by what he sees as a lack of transparency by the Biden administration over the New Jersey drone flap.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Lara Trump says she will not seek to replace Rubio in Senate
item.description: The incoming president announced Rubio as his Secretary of State choice in November. Earlier this month, Lara Trump stepped down as RNC co-chair to “heavily consider” taking on the role in Congress's upper chamber.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Driver killed by police after driving truck into Killeen Mall, at least 5 injured
item.description: A driver was shot and killed by law enforcement officers after driving a trucking into the Killeen Mall Saturday night, injuring at least five people, according to the Texas Department of Public Safety.
item.dc:creator: Cora Neas, NewsNation

item.title: Lara Trump says she's removing herself from consideration to be Florida senator
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Lara Trump, the daughter-in-law of President-elect Donald Trump, said Saturday that she's removing herself from consideration to be a Florida senator — ending speculation that she could replace Republican Sen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rickey Henderson, MLB's all-time stolen base leader, dies at 65: reports
item.description: Rickey Henderson, the man who changed Major League Baseball with his blazing, unbeatable speed, has died, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Blake Lively accuses 'It Ends With Us' director Justin Baldoni of harassment, smear campaign
item.description: In the complaint, Lively accuses Baldoni and the studio of embarking on a “multi-tiered plan” to damage her reputation following a meeting in which she and her husband Ryan Reynolds addressed “repeated sexual harassment and other disturbing behavior” by Baldoni and a producer on the movie.
item.dc:creator: Mallika Sen, KRON4

item.title: What NYC Mayor Adams told Luigi Mangione when they met in NYC
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams said he “wanted to send a strong message” when he met accused UnitedHealthcare CEO killer Luigi Mangione in New York City on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, WGN 9

item.title: Biden signs bill that averts a government shutdown and brings a close to days of Washington upheaval
item.description: President Joe Biden signed a bill into law Saturday that averts a government shutdown, bringing a final close to days of upheaval after Congress approved a temporary funding plan just past the deadline and refused President-elect Donald Trump’s core debt demands in the package.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATT BROWN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Funding saga forecasts future struggles for Speaker Johnson
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) averted a government shutdown, but the days-long rollercoaster as the leaders cycled through multiple plans leaves serious questions about how the Speaker will manage the House GOP under President-elect Trump — and whether he can keep his gavel.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Diddy case: New alleged victim files sexual assault lawsuit
item.description: The list of accusers against Sean "Diddy" Combs has gotten longer, and the latest alleged victim to come forward gave an exclusive interview to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: In a calendar rarity, Hanukkah starts this year on Christmas Day
item.description: Hanukkah, Judaism’s eight-day Festival of Lights, begins this year on Christmas Day, which has only happened four times since 1900. For some rabbis, the intersection of the two religious holidays provides an auspicious occasion for interfaith engagement.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fani Willis disqualification may doom Trump’s Georgia case
item.description: Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis’s (D) disqualification from Georgia's election interference case against President-elect Trump and his allies could mark the beginning of the end of the prosecution.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the 34 Republicans that voted against the funding bill to avoid shutdown
item.description: More than 30 House Republicans voted against the government funding bill to avert a shutdown on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 of the most frustrating health insurer tactics and why they exist
item.description: Even for those with health insurance, coverage does not ensure access to care, much less high-quality and affordable care.
item.dc:creator: Monica S. Aswani Assistant Professor of Health Services Administration, University of Alabama at Birmingham, ABC 4

item.title: 1 dead in small plane crash near home of NFL player Dion Dawkins
item.description: One person died in a small plane crash Thursday morning close to the house of Buffalo Bills offensive lineman Dion Dawkins, police said.
item.dc:creator: Aidan Joly, WGN 9

item.title: Senate passes bill to boost Social Security benefits for some
item.description: The Senate voted overwhelmingly in the early hours of Saturday to pass legislation to boost Social Security benefits for more than 2 million Americans.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms 235th Biden judge, surpassing Trump’s record
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) marked the achievement after the Senate confirmed Serena Raquel Murillo to serve as district judge for the Central District of California.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Indiana man sentenced to the maximum of 130 years in prison for 2017 killings of 2 teenage girls
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — An Indiana man convicted in the 2017 killings of two teenage girls who vanished during a winter hike was sentenced to a maximum of 130 years in prison Friday in the case that’s long cast a shadow over the teens’ small hometown of Delphi. Allen, 52, was convicted on Nov.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Over 600,000 Nuna Rava car seats recalled over possible crash failure
item.description: Nuna Baby Essentials is recalling more than 600,000 of its Rava line of car seats over a harness defect that could cause the restraint system to fail in a crash.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Parking dispute at Southern California mall turns into violent brawl, robbery
item.description: COMMERCE, Calif. (KTLA) - A parking dispute at the popular Citadel Outlets in Commerce turned into a violent, all-out brawl and robbery Wednesday that was caught on a cellphone camera.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Biden canceling student loan debt for 55K public service workers
item.description: President Biden announced on Friday he would be canceling more student debt for tens of thousands of public service workers, possibly his last major move on the issue before leaving office in a month.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Obama reveals his favorite books of 2024
item.description: With 2024 coming to a close, former President Obama is again sharing out a list of his favorites, posting his ten favorite books of the year.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, KRON4

item.title: Tren de Aragua gang members arrested in NYC apartment next to daycare facility
item.description: Several suspected members of Tren De Aragua (TdA) were busted hiding out in a New York City apartment building located next to a daycare center, a law enforcement source tells Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Traveling for the holidays? These are the busiest times to fly or drive
item.description: Drivers and airline passengers without reindeer and sleighs better make a dash for it: It's beginning to look like another record for holiday travel in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: David Koenig, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Government shutdown is averted just after deadline as Congress rejects Trump's debt limit demands
item.description: The Senate has rushed through final passage of a bipartisan plan to fund federal operations and prevent a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Farnoush Amiri And Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Johnson says plan C reached to avert shutdown, vote expected
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said Republicans have arrived at a plan C to avert a shutdown and the House will vote Friday morning on the legislation. "Yeah, yeah, we have a plan," Johnson said Friday morning as he entered the Capitol. "We're expecting votes this morning, so you all stay tuned.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin school shooting by first-semester student lasted 8 minutes: official
item.description: The 15-year-old girl who police say killed a teacher and teen student in a shooting at a Christian school Wisconsin was in her first semester at the school and the attack lasted eight minutes, school officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Starbucks workers in L.A., other major cities, go on strike
item.description: Workers at Starbucks stores started a five-day strike starting Friday to protest lack of progress in contract negotiations with the company. The strikes were scheduled to begin in Los Angeles, Chicago and Seattle and could spread to hundreds of stores across the country by Christmas Eve.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Young nuns: Two diverse paths to a rare life of faith
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Less than 1% of Catholic nuns in the United States today are 30 or younger. Seyram Adzokpa and Zoey Stapleton are two of the young women who have made the rare decision to join a religious community and begin the long process to become nuns. Here are their stories.
item.dc:creator: JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Starbucks workers plan strikes that could hit hundreds of stores by Christmas Eve
item.description: Workers at Starbucks stores plan to go on a five-day strike starting Friday to protest lack of progress in contract negotiations with the company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: More student loans canceled in final round of forgiveness before Biden leaves office
item.description: With the latest round of relief, Biden has now canceled an unprecedented $180 billion in federal student loans through existing programs, covering 4.9 million Americans.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How New York prosecutors used a terrorism law in the charges against Luigi Mangione
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York prosecutors are using a 9/11-era anti-terrorism law in their case against the man charged with gunning down UnitedHealthcare's CEO outside a midtown Manhattan hotel.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
